title: Proposed Mechanism
description: A structural proposal that addresses one or more puzzles. Mechanisms are smaller units than full frameworks: a candidate framework typically composes several mechanisms. Each mechanism declares which puzzles it addresses, which benchmarks it touches, and what new structure it introduces. Compression score = puzzles_closed / parameters_introduced surfaces high-leverage proposals.

id.description: Stable slug used as the filename.

type.description: symmetry = closes a puzzle by introducing or restoring a symmetry (PQ, family symmetries); dynamical = closes via cosmological or field-theoretic evolution (relaxion, leptogenesis); anthropic = closes via environmental selection; topological = closes via topological / vacuum-structure arguments; uv_completion = a UV theory whose IR limit produces the structure (string compactification, asymptotic safety); composite = closes by compositeness or strong dynamics (technicolor, composite Higgs); geometric = closes via geometric / extra-dimensional structure.

summary.description: Plain-English description of the mechanism, 2-5 sentences.

addresses_puzzles.description: Edges into puzzles/. Each entry names a puzzle, the role this mechanism plays for it, and the confidence in that claim.

role.description: solves = closes the puzzle entirely if the mechanism is correct; ameliorates = softens the puzzle but leaves residual tuning; explains_pattern = produces the observed structure of values; requires = the mechanism depends on the puzzle being closed by something else (used to track dependency edges, e.g. leptogenesis requires seesaw).

touches_benchmarks.description: Edges into benchmarks/. Each entry names a benchmark and how the mechanism interacts with it. Used by dot_alignment to flag mechanisms that violate established bounds.

effect.description: respects = comfortably consistent; near_bound = saturates the current limit; violates = mechanism in its naive form is excluded; predicts_signal = mechanism predicts a specific positive signature at this benchmark (the most informative case).

introduces.description: What new structure the mechanism postulates. Used to compute the compression score's denominator.

new_fields.description: New fundamental fields (e.g., 'axion a', 'right-handed neutrinos N_R').

new_scales.description: New mass / energy scales (e.g., 'f_a ~ 10^9-10^12 GeV', 'M_R ~ 10^9-10^14 GeV').

new_symmetries.description: New global, gauge, or discrete symmetries (e.g., 'U(1)_PQ', 'Z_2 mirror').

new_parameters_count.description: Approximate count of new free parameters introduced (couplings, masses, mixing angles, scales not derived from elsewhere). Used as the denominator of the compression score. Be conservative; count parameters not their fitted values.

structural_features.description: Free-tag array used for clustering. Examples: 'discrete_symmetry', 'global_symmetry', 'scalar_at_intermediate_scale', 'extra_dimensions', 'mirror_sector', 'majorana_mass', 'topological_term'.

field_tags.description: Controlled vocabulary describing which kinds of new spacetime-field content this mechanism introduces. Used for shared-content queries across mechanisms. Tag = what the field IS, not what job it does in this mechanism (e.g. an axion-like inflaton gets 'qcd_blind_pseudoscalar', not a separate inflaton tag). Empty array means 'no new spacetime fields' (e.g. anthropic landscape, three-generations anomaly-cancellation arguments).

composes_with.description: Other mechanism ids this can coexist with naturally.

excludes.description: Other mechanism ids this is incompatible with (would conflict over the same puzzle or duplicate structure).

status.description: mainstream = currently a leading proposal; niche = proposed but not dominant; historical = once leading, now superseded; disfavored = empirically pressured but not strictly dead.

history.description: Brief sketch of when the mechanism was proposed and how its evaluation has evolved.
